text: Is my information safe when paying with contactless Visa card? Visa contactless transactions are just as secure as magnetic stripe transactions and are processed through the same reliable payment network. And, because you remain in control of your payment device during Visa contactless transactions, the risk of fraud is reduced. Visa contactless transactions are just as secure as magnetic stripe transactions and are processed through the same reliable payment network. And, because you remain in control of your payment device during Visa contactless transactions, the risk of fraud is reduced. Can my card with contactless technology or my Visa Micro Tag be accidentally read by the secure reader if it’s in my purse or pocket? To authorise a payment, you must tap your card or Visa Micro Tag directly within 2.5 to 5 cm (1 to 2 in) of a secure reader that accepts Visa contactless payments. It also has to be correctly oriented for the transaction to go through. Only readers at authorised merchants can process the information on the Visa Micro Tag and contactless Visa card. How am I protected if my card with contactless Visa card or my Visa Micro Tag is lost or stolen? With Visa’s Zero Liability policy, you will not be held responsible for fraudulent charges or unauthorised purchases made with your contactless Visa card. This means you can use your card to shop with total confidence, as you will always be protected against unauthorised use. This means you can use your card to shop with total confidence, as you will always be protected against unauthorised use. What is the Visa Micro Tag, and how does it work? Visa Micro Tag is Visa’s first payment device designed exclusively for Visa contactless payments. It is far smaller than a traditional card - small enough to hang on your key ring. Shortwave radio technology is embedded inside a durable key
